name: Kindergarten Travel & Global Citizenship — Semester 2
description: An 18-week, play-based Kindergarten pathway for homes and communities around the world, accessibility, and gratitude. Lessons are brief, hands-on, flexible, and designed for adult-guided learning.
learningGoals: Build confidence and curiosity with homes and communities around the world, accessibility, and gratitude.
Communicate learning through talk, drawing, movement, play, or a simple creation.
Practice safe, kind, and responsible participation.

lessons.title: Notice and talk: homes and communities around the world, accessibility, and gratitude
lessons.objective: I can notice details and share a question about homes and communities around the world, accessibility, and gratitude through a close look at homes and communities around the world, accessibility, and gratitude.

lessons.objective: I can notice details and share a question about homes and communities around the world, accessibility, and gratitude through a guided practice with homes and communities around the world, accessibility, and gratitude.

units.title: Wonder and notice: homes and communities around the world, accessibility, and gratitude
units.essentialQuestion: What can we notice and wonder about homes and communities around the world, accessibility, and gratitude?
outcomes: I can notice details and share a question about homes and communities around the world, accessibility, and gratitude.
I can use words, pictures, movement, or materials to communicate my thinking.

activities.title: A close look at homes and communities around the world, accessibility, and gratitude
activities.learnerAction: With an adult or teacher nearby when needed, the learner participates in a close look at homes and communities around the world, accessibility, and gratitude, uses words, pictures, movement, or materials to communicate thinking, and cleans up safely.

successCriteria: The learner participates safely and respectfully in homes and communities around the world, accessibility, and gratitude.
The learner communicates one observation, choice, or question using speech, drawing, movement, or a supported response.
